metric_name: Enterprise management software annual growth rate
notes: Large-scale systems management and network management command lion's share; all segments growing.

metric_name: Market structure — primary product approach divide
metric_value: Framework vs. Best-of-Breed
notes: Today's primary line of demarcation in suppliers' approaches to Enterprise Management.

metric_name: Computer Associates Unicenter TNG market position
metric_value: co-leader of Framework camp
notes: Cross-platform common repository common presentation; Framework proponent.

metric_value: co-leader of Framework camp
notes: TME Framework; cross-platform solutions.

metric_value: leader of Best-of-Breed camp
notes: Building block approach; OpenView flagship of best-of-breed.

metric_value: Framework acceptance will spread but leave plenty of market for best-of-breed

metric_name: Fastest growing segment of enterprise management
metric_value: application management
notes: Management of applications is the fastest growing segment of enterprise management.

metric_value: SLA adoption proliferation creates market for innovative point solutions
notes: Continued trend of SLA adoption will create market opportunity for start-up companies.

metric_value: AI pattern recognition first in network management then systems and application management
notes: Aberdeen expects rise in application of AI technology to management solutions.

metric_name: Enterprise management build vs. buy stance
metric_value: majority from third parties; almost all large enterprises augment with home-grown customization
notes: Too many excellent product possibilities for resource-stretched IS group to justify primarily inhouse strategy.

metric_name: Professional services role in enterprise management
metric_value: few enterprises deploy large-scale management without third-party planning and implementation
notes: System integrators consultants and ISV professional service personnel used extensively in initial stages.

metric_name: Primary buying criterion per Fortune 100 CIOs
metric_value: suitability to task
notes: Aberdeen face-to-face interviews with CIOs of Fortune 100 companies.

metric_value: effective integration, time to value, professional services, product vision
notes: Buyers sacrifice small second-tier functionality for effective integration.

metric_value: increasingly price insensitive at high end
notes: Reflects enterprises' growing realization that success or failure of systems management directly impacts business.

metric_name: Strategic decision-making locus for enterprise management
metric_value: senior IS managers and CIOs for enterprise-wide; LOB managers for narrow products
notes: Wide product suites bought by line of business; large-scale strategic decisions by CIOs.

metric_value: aggressive and innovative product assaults on aging installed base of previous-generation solutions
notes: Aberdeen anticipates aggressive and innovative product and marketing assaults.

metric_value: facing limitations as networks evolve rapidly
notes: SNMP lowest common denominator; designed to cover only basics of TCP/IP; hitting limits.

metric_value: 99%+ as minimum standard of acceptable service
notes: Businesses demanding higher-than-ever network availability; raising bar for management software.

metric_value: four levels: none / screen-level / event-level / business-centric
notes: Business-centric integration still largely in the future; requires massive cultural changes.

metric_value: requires massive cultural changes not just additional technology
notes: Achievement of business-based integration will require more than additional technology.

metric_name: Role of partnerships in enterprise management market
metric_value: critical for success; no supplier can be all things to all organizations
notes: Framework giants partner with specialized products; best-of-breed vendors partner with each other.
